title: CI: run rootless tests under ubuntu
body:
Reason: to catch errors before they surface in RHEL.

One of the Ubuntus is specially crafted to run with cgroups v1
and runc. Although this isn't quite the same as RHEL, it's as
close as we can come in our CI environment, and I suspect it
would have caught #10234 (a regression).

Sorry, team.

[NO TESTS NEEDED] - we already have tests, this PR will make them run

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Additions are marked with ellipse outline
![cirrus-map-ci_rootless_add_ubuntu](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/117213493-e6a39500-adb8-11eb-8534-1e81369db1b2.png)
Oh wow, good catch. Almost a duplicate: differences are (1) I intend to run system tests in addition to integration tests, and (2) I intend to devote myself to merging this urgently, ASAP.

>  merging this urgently, ASAP.

@edsantiago since #9905 is dedicated to integration tests, what about limiting this PR to only the system-tests?  Reason being, my PR is blocked by [a tricky 'podman cp' issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9997) buried in buildah code.  Based on experience so far, I do not see that issue being fixed "urgently, ASAP" :disappointed: So I think if you limit this PR to just system-tests there can be some realized testing benefit while I wait for a fix.

I am just going to merge if it passes
/lgtm
/approve
/hold

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> I want this merged today.

I understand, but it's not worth subjecting people to random failures of `Podman cp` tests!

> if it passes

Clarification: Issue #9997 is a high-likelihood flake.  I'm not opposed to having a `skip()` added to all the `podman cp` tests to avoid it...but that has consequences: Hiding MORE regressions on Ubuntu until #9997 is fixed (i.e. `podman cp` and `buildah copy` are very popular).

@cevich if I understand your concern correctly, you believe that enabling rootless integration tests on ubuntu (not clear if 2010 or 2104) will result in a high number of flakes due to #9997. Is that correct?

If that's your concern, I will still make a case for merging my PR ASAP. First: #9905 has languished for over a month. In that time, we've had a regression that broke RHEL, that should have been caught. I want to prevent regressions, and I want to do so right now, today. Second: flakes that don't affect anyone don't get fixed. So, counterintuitively, I **want** flakes. I want people to suffer, because suffering is the only way to motivate people to fix flakes. #9997 has languished a long time too. If we start suffering because CI starts failing, the pressure will be on to find and fix it. (And, third, at the time I write this, integration tests have passed on this PR. They also passed 100% on my previous submission. Maybe #9997 is somehow already fixed by another commit?)

I will honor your `/hold`, and will not be the one to release it. I would appreciate an independent team member's resolution on this.

(And if I've misunderstood your concern, I apologize, and would welcome clarification)

> Maybe #9997 is somehow already fixed by another commit?

It's certainly possible.  It's also easily possible the flake-rate changed due to timing changes.  

>  I want people to suffer, because suffering is the only way to motivate people to fix flakes.

While arguably effective psychological manipulation in some cases, I don't believe it's helpful as leverage for a technical decision. My opinion on that aside, we have PR's blocked by issues for a substantially similar reason:  It brings more eyeballs to a problem.

Worse...now that (apparently) the flake-rate has reduced, merging this (and by extension #9905) would actually ***LOWER*** visibility.  That would leave us with zero blocked PRs and a [hard to reproduce] issue lying around, among a hundred others to be ignored.

> misunderstood your concern

That's also part of the issue...it's an unintentional cross-device access race affecting podman and buildah, with not well understood implications - including security.  There are device-locks involved due to this access, so it's possible for this fault to negatively affect other system services and containers.

The copy problem also affects Fedora (and every other distro), we simply haven't observed it there yet.

In any case...my point is, I don't know what the implications are of ignoring #9997.  I'm open to adding a `skip()` or ignoring the problem (and merging) if the experts deem it unworthy of holding up new testing.

Lastly if you care to take a peep yourself, the [error is coming from this line](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/copier/copier.go#L1169).  It seems to be caused by a TOCTOU race on an in-copy-context symlink pointing to an out-of-context cross-device file, when the file disappears (or otherwise becomes inaccessible) immediately prior to being lstat()'ed.

>I don't know what the implications are of ignoring #9997

I'm sorry, I must not have communicated properly. My intention is not to ignore that flake, it is to embrace it: give it higher visibility. Make it fail often, collect data in my flake tracker, see what patterns emerge that might help identify and fix it. (And, of course, provide stronger motivation to do so).

> I'm sorry, I must not have communicated properly.

Nope you did fine, I understand the intent.  I should probably call the underlying problem a "race" not a "flake", since it's not completely innocent.  After looking and understanding the code more fully...there are indeed at least two TOCTOU races we happened by luck/accident to hit in the podman tests.

But I'm worried since it seems to be reproducing ***less*** now, if we merge these PRs then the problem may become easier to ignore - difficult to reproduce and lost in the shuffle of issues.

[In any case, I think Nalin and I are honing in on a fix.](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9997#issuecomment-833860160).  Once it's addressed in buildah, it's just a quick (hopefully) re-vendor to bring it over here.

Hmmm....taking a step back...My motivation to not merging new testing with possible random breakage is to spare humans the agony of debugging.  If the problem is reproducing less now (for whatever reason) that does mean the risk of annoying humble humans is also probably lower.

How about let's do this:  I trust Nalin will figure something out eventually, and buildah will get fixed.  Let's give until Monday afternoon to sort it out, and if there are significant snags (like buildah test failures or problems vendoring into podman) we can just merge this and cross fingers.

Could that be a compromise?

/hold cancel
Well see how the weekend goes.
